posts.title: Introduction to PLC Programming for Controls Engineers
posts.author: Admin
posts.excerpt: A practical introduction to PLC programming concepts every controls engineer should know.
posts.content:
What is a PLC?

A Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) is an industrial digital computer used for automation of electromechanical processes. Unlike general-purpose computers, PLCs are designed for multiple input/output arrangements, extended temperature ranges, and resistance to vibration and impact.

Key Programming Languages

  • Ladder Diagram (LD) – Most widely used. Resembles electrical relay logic diagrams.
  • Function Block Diagram (FBD) – Graphical language using blocks and wires.
  • Structured Text (ST) – High-level language similar to Pascal.
  • Instruction List (IL) – Low-level assembly-like language.

Getting Started

Start with Ladder Logic — it is the most common and best supported across all major PLC platforms including SIEMENS, Allen Bradley, and Mitsubishi.

posts.title: HMI Design Best Practices — ISA-101 Guidelines
posts.author: Admin
posts.excerpt: Follow ISA-101 standards to design effective, operator-friendly HMI screens.
posts.content:
Why ISA-101?

ISA-101 defines a standard for Human Machine Interfaces in process control systems. Following these guidelines reduces operator errors and improves situational awareness.

Key Principles

  • Use a gray background — not black or white
  • Reserve red for alarms only
  • Use high-contrast colors only for abnormal states
  • Organize screens in a 4-level hierarchy: Overview → Unit → Equipment → Detail

Color Coding

Standard ISA-101 colors: Gray = normal, Red = alarm/trip, Yellow = warning, Green = running/on, Blue = setpoint/manual.
